overall_result: CLEAN — zero undefined-behaviour findings across three orthogonal configurations
scope_included: lib tests (360 with std+serde, 351 with no_std)
alloc-backed paths (VecDeque, String, etc. when std feature is on)

scope_excluded_and_why.integration_tests: tests/long_duration_stability.rs, tests/bit_exactness.rs, tests/concurrency_observer.rs, tests/proptest_invariants.rs are 1-million-sample end-to-end simulations. Native runtime is seconds; under Miri MIR interpretation this is estimated at tens of hours to a full day. They exercise the same module code paths already covered by the lib Miri passes. Excluded for runtime feasibility, not for defects.
scope_excluded_and_why.doc_tests: Doc tests build examples from rustdoc snippets; these are exercised by cargo test --doc under native execution and are not Miri-gated because the crate forbids unsafe code at compile time.

what_miri_validates: Undefined behaviour from integer-overflow in debug
Aliasing-model violations (even though forbid(unsafe_code) eliminates most UB)
what_miri_does_not_validate: Arithmetic overflow in release mode (Rust intentionally does not trap)
non_certification_notice: This audit is evidence for reviewers, not a certification. DSFB does not claim compliance with IEC, ISO, RTCA, MIL, NIST, or any other safety standard. The audit confirms that the lib test surface of dsfb-rf@1.0.0 executes without Miri-detectable undefined behaviour under the configurations listed above.
